title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for http semantics, resources, methods, preconditions, and status and demands the smallest safe resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
